Class Mat4Transform

java.lang.Object
gmaths.Mat4Transform
public final class Mat4Transform extends Object
A static class for a set of methods to create transformation matrices. The matrix is represented in row-column form. Matrices are formed so as to be used in premultiplication of vectors, i.e. matrix x vector.

    Method
    Description
    static Mat4
    lookAt(Vec3 from, Vec3 to, Vec3 worldup)
    Creates a view matrix corresponding to a camera at a given position, looking at a particular target point, with a specified worldup direction.
    static Mat4
    perspective(float fov, float aspect)
    Creates a perspective matrix with near clip plane at 0.1f and far clip plane at 100f.
    static Mat4
    perspective(float fov, float aspect, float near, float far)
    Creates a perspective matrix.
    static Mat4
    rotateAroundX(float angle)
    Creates a 4x4 rotation matrix to rotate around the X axis.
    static Mat4

    Creates a 4x4 rotation matrix to rotate around the Y axis.

    Creates a 4x4 rotation matrix to rotate around the Z axis.

  • Method Details

    • translate

      public static Mat4 translate(Vec3 v)
      Creates a 4x4 translation matrix
      Parameters:
      v - The translation amount (x,y,z)
      Returns:
      The resulting 4x4 translation matrix, as a Mat4

    • translate

      public static Mat4 translate(float tx, float ty, float tz)
      Creates a 4x4 translation matrix
      Parameters:
      tx - The translation amount for x
      ty - The translation amount for y
      tz - The translation amount for z
      Returns:
      The resulting 4x4 translation matrix, as a Mat4

    • scale

      public static Mat4 scale(Vec3 v)
      Creates a 4x4 scale matrix
      Parameters:
      v - The scale amount (x,y,z)
      Returns:
      The resulting 4x4 scale matrix, as a Mat4

    • scale

      public static Mat4 scale(float sx, float sy, float sz)
      Creates a 4x4 scale matrix
      Parameters:
      sx - The scale amount for x
      sy - The scale amount for y
      sz - The scale amount for z
      Returns:
      The resulting 4x4 scale matrix, as a Mat4

    • rotateAroundX

      public static Mat4 rotateAroundX(float angle)
      Creates a 4x4 rotation matrix to rotate around the X axis.
      Parameters:
      angle - The amount of rotation in degrees. Positive values indicate anticlockwise rotation.
      Returns:
      The resulting 4x4 rotation matrix, as a Mat4

    • rotateAroundY

      public static Mat4 rotateAroundY(float angle)
      Creates a 4x4 rotation matrix to rotate around the Y axis.
      Parameters:
      angle - The amount of rotation in degrees. Positive values indicate anticlockwise rotation.
      Returns:
      The resulting 4x4 rotation matrix, as a Mat4

    • rotateAroundZ

      public static Mat4 rotateAroundZ(float angle)
      Creates a 4x4 rotation matrix to rotate around the Z axis.
      Parameters:
      angle - The amount of rotation in degrees. Positive values indicate anticlockwise rotation.
      Returns:
      The resulting 4x4 rotation matrix, as a Mat4

    • perspective

      public static Mat4 perspective(float fov, float aspect)
      Creates a perspective matrix with near clip plane at 0.1f and far clip plane at 100f.
      Parameters:
      fov - The field of view for the perspective. Default is 45.
      aspect - The aspect ratio of the display area, which equals width/height, where width and height are floating point values.
      Returns:
      The resulting perspective matrix, as a Mat4

    • perspective

      public static Mat4 perspective(float fov, float aspect, float near, float far)
      Creates a perspective matrix.
      Parameters:
      fov - The field of view for the perspective. Default is 45f.
      aspect - The aspect ratio of the display area, which equals width/height, where width and height are floating point values.
      near - The distance of the near clip plane. Default is 0.1f;
      far - The distance of the far clip plane. Default is 100f.
      Returns:
      The resulting perspective matrix, as a Mat4

    • lookAt

      public static Mat4 lookAt(Vec3 from, Vec3 to, Vec3 worldup)
      Creates a view matrix corresponding to a camera at a given position, looking at a particular target point, with a specified worldup direction. The worldup direction will be changed using the Gram-Schmidt process to be perpendicular to the vector joining the position to the target.
      Parameters:
      from - The camera postion.
      to - The target that the camera is looking at.
      worldup - The up direction for the world.
      Returns:
      The 4x4 viewing matrix, as a Mat4.
